findings.question: Q1 — Pre-selection flow: does setActiveOgham(lairOgham) survive startRun() reset?
evidence: main.ts line 437: store.getState().startRun(chosenBiome) — this resets run state including activeOgham to ''
main.ts lines 439-441: if (lairResult.lairOgham) { store.getState().setActiveOgham(lairResult.lairOgham); } — called AFTER startRun, so it overwrites the reset value correctly
Order is: startRun() → setActiveOgham(lairOgham) — the described invariant holds
findings.notes: The comment on line 438 matches the implementation. No regression risk here.

findings.question: Q2 — Does state().run.activeOgham in gameLoop read the pre-set value correctly?
evidence: gameLoop line 498: const preSelected = state().run.activeOgham;
state is store.getState (line 464: const state = store.getState;) — this is a reference to the getter function, called as state() each time, so it always reads current store state
By the time gameLoop runs its first card cycle, startRun+setActiveOgham have already completed synchronously in main(), so state().run.activeOgham holds the lair-pre-selected value
findings.notes: No stale closure issue. state is not captured once — it is called fresh each loop iteration.

findings.question: Q3 — Is hideOghamPanel() still called after oghamChoice is used?
evidence: gameLoop line 506: hideOghamPanel() — called unconditionally after the ogham block, regardless of whether oghamChoice came from preSelected or from showOghamPanel()

When preSelected is truthy, showOghamPanel() is never called (short-circuit ||), so there is no open overlay to hide, but hideOghamPanel() is idempotent (checks overlay exists, resolveChoice is null — no side effects)
hideOghamPanel() also resolves any dangling promise (BUG-C58-03 fix) — safe to call even if panel was never shown
findings.notes: No handler leak risk. escapeHandler cleanup inside hideOghamPanel() is also safe when escapeHandler is null.

findings.question: Q4 — Z-index conflict: showOghamPanel() from crystal zone while lair canvas is shown?
evidence: lair-canvas-wrapper z-index: 10 (main.ts line 223)
ogham-panel-overlay z-index: 200 (OghamPanel.ts line 32)
Both are position:fixed, so z-index stacking is relative to the root stacking context — OghamPanel (200) renders above the lair canvas (10) as intended
The OghamPanel overlay covers the entire viewport (inset:0) and has role=dialog + aria-modal=true, so interaction with the lair behind it is blocked visually and semantically
findings.notes: No risk. The 190-unit z-index gap is more than sufficient. The lair's own biome-picker overlay is z-index:30 (main.ts line 155) — still well below 200.

findings.question: Q5 — Does the stochasticTimer rename compile cleanly? Any missed usages?
evidence: AmbientSession interface (SFXManager.ts line 188): stochasticTimer: ReturnType<typeof setTimeout> | null — correctly renamed
scheduleRainDrops (line 284): session.stochasticTimer = setTimeout(...) — uses new name
scheduleBirds (line 304): session.stochasticTimer = setTimeout(...) — uses new name
stopAmbient (line 423): if (session.stochasticTimer !== null) { clearTimeout(session.stochasticTimer); session.stochasticTimer = null; } — uses new name
startAmbient (line 379): stochasticTimer: null in session literal — uses new name
No occurrence of the old name 'birdTimer' found in the file — rename is complete and consistent across all 5 usages
findings.notes: The comment on stopAmbient (line 422) still reads '// Cancel bird timer' — this is a minor comment staleness, not a compile issue. Functionally correct.

findings.question: Q6 — TypeScript errors or type mismatches from the return type change?
findings.verdict: ONE MINOR COMMENT STALENESS — NO TYPE ERROR
evidence: runMerlinLair return type: Promise<{ biomeId: string; lairOgham: string | null }> — declared on line 217
main() destructures correctly: const lairResult = await runMerlinLair(app) then lairResult.biomeId and lairResult.lairOgham (lines 392-440)
lairResult.lairOgham is typed string | null — the null guard (if lairResult.lairOgham)) on line 439 is correct before calling setActiveOgham()
showOghamPanel() returns Promise<string | null> — assigned to lairSelectedOgham: string | null — types align
No implicit any. No missing null checks on the critical path.
MINOR: stopAmbient comment at line 422 still says '// Cancel bird timer' — should be 'Cancel stochastic timer'. Non-breaking.
findings.notes: TypeScript type flow is clean end-to-end. The old Promise<string> callers no longer exist — the function was only called from main() which was updated in the same commit.

issues.issue: stopAmbient() comment at SFXManager.ts line 422 says '// Cancel bird timer' — stale after birdTimer → stochasticTimer rename
issues.recommendation: Update comment to '// Cancel stochastic timer' for consistency with the renamed field

correct_behaviors: setActiveOgham called after startRun — reset sequence is correct
state() called fresh each loop iteration — no stale closure on activeOgham
hideOghamPanel() called unconditionally — no escape handler leak
OghamPanel z-index 200 safely above lair z-index 10 — no conflict
stochasticTimer rename complete across all 5 usages in SFXManager.ts
Return type change from Promise<string> to Promise<{biomeId,lairOgham}> is correctly consumed at the single call site in main()
